articles.name: Announcing goal conversion tracking in Explore
articles.title: Announcing goal conversion tracking in Explore
articles.body:
We’re excited to announce the next evolution of goals for messaging: goal tracking and reporting in Explore. This release brings goal conversions into Zendesk Explore, so you can understand which conversations drive outcomes and why..

This announcement includes the following sections:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Goals for messaging are now reportable in Explore, allowing both high-level and in-depth analysis of goal conversions across messaging conversations, to help you make sure that goal tracking aligns with existing Explore workflows and reporting standards.

With this release, you can:

  • View conversion performance directly from the Goals conversions tab in the Zendesk Messaging dashboard in Explore.
  • Analyze goal outcomes using Explore reports, including:
    • Total conversions
    • Messaging conversion rate
    • Total transaction value (where applicable)
    • Satisfaction score for tickets with goal conversions
    • Average conversion time
  • Filter and slice goal performance by attributes such as goal name, agent, group, brand, satisfaction rating, and conversion time brackets.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

Now that goals and conversion tracking in messaging are generally available, customers expect more than confirmation that a goal occurred; they want outcome clarity.

For CX and e-commerce teams, that means answering questions like:

  • Which conversations led to conversions?
  • How did outcomes vary by agent, brand, or group?
  • What did successful conversations have in common?

By bringing goals into Explore, Zendesk helps customers to connect conversational activity to measurable business outcomes. This is a foundational step toward more advanced attribution and conversational commerce reporting.

What do I need to do?

No action is required to start using goal reporting in Explore. The feature is automatically available on all accounts using messaging.

To get value from this release:

  • Review and configure y messaging goals to ensure they reflect meaningful success criteria
  • Explore the new goal-related metrics and attributes in Explore
  • Incorporate goal conversion insights into your regular reporting and performance reviews

See our updated help center articles for more information:

  • Overview of the Zendesk Messaging dashboard
  • Analyzing your messaging tickets
  • Metrics and attributes for Zendesk messaging: Goal conversions dataset

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: My active scorecards don’t show in conversation or assignment filters
articles.title: My active scorecards don’t show in conversation or assignment filters
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

My active scorecards don’t show in conversation or assignment filters. It only displays old ones that I deleted.

Resolution steps

When you create a conversation filter, it displays all scorecards used before, even older ones that are inactive. However, a workspace mismatch usually explains why an active scorecard doesn't appear in your filter list.

Ensure the scorecard belongs to your workspace. Scorecards in Workspace A don’t appear when you filter conversations in Workspace B. If you filter conversations in the Workspace Outlier, you see only scorecards assigned to Outlier. Check the workspace assignment of the scorecard that doesn't appear. For example, if a scorecard such as Bot QA is in the Workspace Automations, it appears in the filter menu only when you work in that workspace.

articles.name: Announcing the ability to import forecast scenarios in WFM (EAP)
articles.title: Announcing the ability to import forecast scenarios in WFM (EAP)
articles.body:
Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM) is excited to announce the Early Access Program (EAP) for importing forecast scenarios.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

The import feature in Forecast scenarios lets you bring your own forecasted values into WFM forecast via CSV uploads. Two import options are available:

  • Forecasted inbound volume import: You can upload your inbound volume forecasts, and Zendesk WFM will use those values as input to its staffing calculations.

  • Forecasted inbound volume and required staffing import: You can upload inbound volume forecasts and final staffing numbers directly when you want to own both forecasting and capacity planning.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

If you have highly specific forecast and staffing calculation, aggregation, and rounding rules, you can now bring those trusted numbers into Zendesk WFM Forecast. By importing your customized inbound volumes and final staffing data directly into Forecast scenarios, you eliminate the need for manual reconciliation and preserve your existing workflows while taking advantage of other Zendesk WFM capabilities, such as auto‑scheduling and reporting. This reduces manual work, increases efficiency, speeds decision‑making, and lowers overhead.

What do I need to do?

If you’d like to participate in the EAP, sign up here. We’ll review sign ups on a regular basis and notify you via email about when Import forecasts will be activated in your account if you're eligible to participate.

To learn more see Importing forecast scenarios (EAP).

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Announcing Form Messages on Social Channels and new Field Types
articles.title: Announcing Form Messages on Social Channels and new Field Types
articles.body:
We are excited to announce that form messages are now supported across all of our social messaging channels. Specifically, this means that you can now launch structured data capture steps for conversational flows over your social channels. This includes being able to use the “ask for details” step in our AI Agents Essentials answers!

Additionally, in order to help our customers to continue their transition from our Chat product to Messaging, we’ve added support and validation for decimal and integer field types to our forms. Two highly requested field types.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

Previously, form messages were only supported by our Web Widget and Mobile SDKs and only supported email, select, and text field types. We have now extended form message support across our social messaging channels and expanded the list of supported field types to include integer and decimal.

Why is Zendesk making this change?

This long-overdue enhancement was heavily requested by customers looking to conduct structured pre-chat data capture in Zendesk. As such, we wanted to introduce a solution that allows our customers to be able to gather data in a structured way regardless of the channel they use.

What do I need to do?

Your next steps depend on how you plan to leverage this new release:

AI Agent Essentials: Add the “ask for details” step to your “answer” and publish that agent on a social channel.
AI Agents Advanced or directly via the Sunshine Conversations API: Follow our API spec to add a form message to your conversational flow or create a Sunshine Conversations template and use that to send the form.
Agent Workspace: Create a Sunshine Conversations template and optionally store this as a macro to easily send a form at any time.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Why can't I find a handled points per hour metric?
articles.title: Why can't I find a handled points per hour metric?
articles.body:
Question

I can't find a handled points per hour metric. I only see solved points per hour. I need a solved points metric and a solved points per hour metric. How can I get these?

Answer

There isn't a handled points per hour metric. As an alternative, you can create an hourly report to show the handled points per hour. For all available metrics, see WFM custom report metrics.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-02-13
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-02-13
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Support
  • Admin Center
  • Copilot
  • AI Agents and Knowledge
  • Zendesk QA
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Products with no updates this week

Support

New:

  • Search now has more granular filters in the suggested filters in the search menu. Click the high-level filters to access more.

Fixed:

  • Requests to GET /api/v2/users/me.json with invalid credentials now return 401 Unauthorized (previously returned 200 OK).

  • Corrected an issue with follow-up tickets so that they inherit the support address value from the previous ticket, so that emails will be sent from the expected address.

  • Corrected two issues with printing tickets. Uppercase characters were printing as lowercase, and large blank areas were being rendered.

Admin Center

New:

Expanded the EAP for unified navigation to now include non-agent workspace accounts, and added analytics to the EAP so it now reflects the unified navigation experience. 

Fixed:

Support

  • Restored type-ahead search in the profile menu within unified navigation.
  • Fixed icons that were disappearing in sidebar navigation.
  • Fixed an issue where agents couldn’t drag and drop certain attachment types into a side conversation in the context panel when unified navigation was enabled.

Admin Center

  • Improved Admin Center search by reducing fuzzy matches for section headers and subheaders.
  • Fixed an issue that would cause banners to appear duplicated in Admin Center when the EAP was enabled.

Knowledge

  • Fixed an issue in Knowledge admin where the save button and revision status could disappear when switching languages.

Copilot

New:

  • Admins and agents can now view certain auto assist events in the ticket events log in the Agent Workspace.

Fixed:

  • Intelligent triage: Improved visibility of Intents conflicts by analyzing intents by showing a loading in the intents list

AI Agents and Knowledge

New:

  • Subdomain Connection with AIAA and Email Integration is now based on the system user instead of the authorized user, as previously seen. Connection of accounts will be based on the main account ID/subdomain
  • Starting February 10, 2026, customers who buy AI agents – Advanced will have their advanced messaging AI agents automatically connected to Zendesk Messaging. The integration is configured based on the account subdomain, a messaging group is automatically created, and new messaging AI agents are automatically added to that group. Any subsequent AI Agents can be added by manually creating an AI agent and assigning the required organization.
    The Object type is not supported in the AI agents - Advanced integration builder.
  • A powerful no-code tool for implementing generative search is now available. Knowledge admins can now leverage quick answers in their help center theme without writing or modifying code.

Fixed:

  • Fixed a bug that prevented cloning within the same organization

Zendesk QA

New:

  • Added a new category to the calibration dashboard

Fixed:

  • Fixed a time tracking error when dual write fails

Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)

New 

  • Forecast Scenarios: Customers can create customizable sets of forecast parameters to experiment with multiple configurations, compare their results, and select the most suitable one as the active scenario.

Products with no updates this week

  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Messaging and live chat
  • Voice
  • Analytics
  • App Marketplace

articles.name: Troubleshooting 401 and 403 Errors on the Zendesk Developer Platform
articles.title: Troubleshooting 401 and 403 Errors on the Zendesk Developer Platform
articles.body:
If you are building on the Zendesk Developer Platform, chances are you have run into 401 or 403 errors at some point. These two status codes account for a large percentage of failed API requests we see from developers, especially early in an integration’s lifecycle.

Although both errors are related to access control, they fail for very different reasons and require different debugging approaches. Treating them as interchangeable often leads to wasted time and repeated failed requests.

In this post, we will walk through how 401 and 403 errors work on Zendesk, how to diagnose them quickly, and how to fix them with confidence using practical examples and troubleshooting steps.

What you will learn

By the end of this post, you will understand:

  • The difference between 401 Unauthorized and 403 Forbidden on Zendesk
  • How to authenticate correctly using API tokens, OAuth access tokens, and JWTs
  • How OAuth scopes affect authorization
  • How to diagnose malformed headers, incorrect subdomains, and expired credentials

 

Understanding 401 vs 403

While both status codes relate to access control, they fail at different stages of request processing.

401 Unauthorized

A 401 response means the Zendesk API could not authenticate the request. Zendesk was unable to determine who the caller is, so it does not attempt to evaluate permissions or business logic.

Common causes include:

  • Missing or malformed Authorization headers
  • Incorrect Base64 encoding for Basic Authentication
  • Incorrect email and token formatting
  • Expired or revoked API tokens
  • Using OAuth tokens in a Basic Auth header
  • Invalid or expired JWTs for Messaging

If you receive a 401 error, focus first on how the request is authenticated, not what the request is trying to do.

403 Forbidden

A 403 response means the request was authenticated successfully, but the authenticated identity does not have permission to perform the requested action.

Typical causes include:

  • OAuth tokens missing required scopes
  • Using end-user credentials to call agent-only endpoints
  • Attempting to access resources belonging to another brand
  • IP restrictions enabled on the account
  • Suspended or downgraded agent accounts

If you receive a 403 error, authentication is working correctly. The issue is authorization.

 

A quick diagnostic flow

When debugging access issues, the fastest way forward is to isolate the problem step by step.

Start by testing your request with curl. If the curl request fails, the issue is likely with credentials or account configuration, not your application code.

Next, confirm that you are calling the correct Zendesk subdomain. Credentials are scoped to a specific environment, and sandbox and production tokens are not interchangeable.

Then, verify that you are using the correct authentication method. Mixing Basic Auth, OAuth, and JWT authentication is a common source of failure.

Check the role of the authenticated user. Many endpoints require agent or admin permissions, even if authentication succeeds.

If you are using OAuth, confirm that the token includes the scopes required by the endpoint.

Finally, consider where the request is running. Browser-origin requests are often blocked by Cross Browser Resource Sharing (CORS) policies when using API token Basic Auth or other unsupported client-side flows. If you need to call the API from a browser, use an OAuth flow that supports CORS, or route requests through a backend service or a Zendesk App using the ZAF client. For more information on CORS requests, see Making client-side CORS requests to the Ticketing API. 

 

Authenticating correctly

Authentication happens before any permission or business logic checks. If authentication fails, Zendesk cannot associate the request with a user or integration and returns a 401 error.

Zendesk supports multiple authentication methods, each with strict formatting requirements.

API token authentication

API tokens use Basic Authentication, where the username includes the /token suffix.

The correct format is:

curl -v \
  -u "agent@example.com/token:YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  "https://your_subdomain.zendesk.com/api/v2/tickets.json"

A common mistake that results in a 401 error is omitting the /token suffix:

emailAddress:APITOKEN

In Node.js, a working example looks like this:

import fetch from "node-fetch";
import btoa from "btoa";

const subdomain = "your_subdomain";
const email = "agent@example.com";
const token = process.env.API_TOKEN;

const response = await fetch(
  `https://${subdomain}.zendesk.com/api/v2/users/me.json`,
  {
    headers: {
      'Authorization': 'Basic ' + btoa(`${email}/token:${token}`),
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
  }
);

console.log(response.status, await response.text());

 

OAuth authentication

OAuth access tokens are commonly used for integrations that require long-lived credentials or fine-grained permission control. OAuth tokens must be sent using the Bearer authentication scheme. The correct header format is:

Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN

Example request using curl:

curl \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  "https://your_subdomain.zendesk.com/api/v2/users/me.json"

In Node.js, a working example looks like this:

import fetch from "node-fetch";

const subdomain = process.env.SUBDOMAIN;
const token = process.env.OAUTH_TOKEN;

const url = `https://${subdomain}.zendesk.com/api/v2/users/me.json`;

const response = await fetch(url, {
  method: "GET",
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`
  }
});

console.log(response.status, await response.text());

A common cause of 401 Unauthorized errors when using OAuth is sending a valid access token with the wrong authentication scheme. In this case, Zendesk is unable to authenticate the request and returns a 401 before evaluating scopes or permissions.

OAuth tokens also introduce the concept of scopes, which explicitly define what actions the token is allowed to perform. A token may authenticate successfully but still return a 403 Forbidden response if it lacks the scopes required by the endpoint being called.

For example, a token with tickets:read can fetch ticket data but cannot create or update tickets. Attempting a write operation without the appropriate scope will consistently result in a 403 error. 

403: You do not have access to this resource

If this occurs, authentication is working as expected, but the token must be regenerated with the correct scopes. For more information on scopes, see OAuth Tokens for Grant Types.

 

JWT authentication for Messaging

When implementing Zendesk Messaging in web or mobile applications, JWT authentication is used to securely identify end users. In this model, your backend is responsible for generating and signing a JSON Web Token (JWT) using the secret configured in Admin Center. Zendesk validates the token before allowing the Messaging session to be associated with a user.

Unlike API tokens or OAuth, Messaging JWTs require specific claims and header information that allow Zendesk to resolve the user’s identity correctly.

At a minimum, a Messaging JWT must include:

  • kid – The Key ID from the Zendesk Admin Center. This must be included in the JWT header, not the payload.
  • external_id – A unique identifier for the user. Zendesk uses this value as the primary key when matching or creating user records.
  • scope – Must be set to "user", indicating that the token is intended for end-user authentication in Messaging.

Additional claims such as name, email, and email_verified are optional and can be included to populate user details in the agent interface or support email-based identity matching.


Example Node.js JWT generator: 

import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";
const payload = {
  scope: "user",
  external_id: "user_12345",
  name: "Jane Doe",
  exp: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + (5 * 60)
};

const token = jwt.sign(payload, process.env.ZENDESK_JWT_SECRET, {
	keyid: process.env.ZENDESK_KEYID,
});

console.log(token);

If required claims such as external_id or scope are missing, or if the token is signed with the wrong secret, Zendesk will return a 401 Unauthorized response.


Common causes of JWT-related 401 errors include:

  • Using a JWT secret from the wrong environment (sandbox vs production)
  • Sending an expired token or one with invalid time-based claims
  • Omitting required claims such as external_id or scope 
  • Signing the token with an incorrect or rotated secret
  • Sending a malformed or improperly encoded JWT

When debugging Messaging authentication issues, first confirm that the token is signed with the correct secret and includes the required claims. If authentication succeeds but user identity behavior is unexpected, inspect the values used for external_id and any optional identity fields to ensure they are stable and consistent across sessions. For more information see Authenticating users in your application.
 

Common causes of 401 errors

A 401 response means Zendesk could not authenticate the request and could not determine the caller’s identity.

This is most often caused by incorrectly constructed Authorization headers, disabled tokens, or environment mismatches.

Basic Authentication headers must be formatted as below, or the equivalent:

headers: {
      'Authorization': 'Basic ' + btoa(`${email}/token:${token}`),
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}

Unexpected characters, whitespace, or encoding issues frequently cause silent failures.

Finally, remember that the Zendesk REST API does not support browser-origin authentication. Client-side JavaScript requests will fail due to CORS, missing session cookies, and unsupported authentication flows. Use a backend service or a Zendesk App built with the ZAF client instead.

 

Common causes of 403 errors

A 403 response indicates that Zendesk authenticated the request, but denied access due to permission rules.

The most common cause is missing OAuth scopes. For example, a token with ticket:read can fetch tickets but cannot create or update them. Write attempts consistently return a 403 error.

OAuth scopes cannot be modified after token creation. If scopes are incorrect, a new token must be generated.

Another common source of confusion is attempting to call agent-only endpoints using end-user credentials. End-user OAuth tokens may work for /users/me, but will return 403 when calling restricted endpoints such as tickets, views, or ticket fields.

Other frequent causes include revoked tokens, IP allowlisting restrictions, and multi-brand access limitations. In these cases, the request is rejected either because the credentials are no longer active or because the user fails to meet specific access criteria.

 

A step-by-step troubleshooting approach

1. Validate credentials in isolation When in doubt, start by removing your application code from the equation. Validate your credentials using curl :

curl -v \
  -u "email/token:token" \
  "https://your_subdomain.zendesk.com/api/v2/users/me.json"
  • If this fails: The issue is likely a credential or an account related issue.
  • If this succeeds: The credentials are valid. The issue lies within your application logic or environment. Move to step 2.

2. Check for CORS limitations: If the curl command works but your client-side application fails, you are likely hitting CORS restrictions.

Open your browser's developer console and inspect the error. If you see a 401/403 error accompanied by a console message regarding Access-Control-Allow-Origin, the browser is blocking the request before Zendesk can process it.

3. Inspect the raw request headers If you are running server-side code (Node.js, Python, PHP, etc.) and still facing issues:.

  • Log the Authorization header: Print the exact string your application is sending. Ensure there are no hidden whitespace characters or incorrect prefixes (e.g., ensuring Basic vs Bearer is used correctly).
  • Verify Environment: Confirm that your application is targeting the correct subdomain. It is common to accidentally target a sandbox URL with production credentials, or vice versa.

4. Verify Scopes and Claims If authentication is successful but you receive a 403 Forbidden, your token may lack the necessary permissions.

  • For OAuth: Verify your current scopes by calling the /api/v2/oauth/tokens/current endpoint. Ensure the token has the scope required for the resource you are trying to access.

  • For Messaging/JWT: Revalidate your JWT payload. Ensure the kid (Key ID) matches your Zendesk configuration and that the signing secret used to generate the token is correct.

     

Final thoughts

Most 401 and 403 errors on the Zendesk Developer Platform stem from a small number of predictable misconfigurations. Once you clearly separate authentication failures from authorization failures, debugging becomes significantly faster and more reliable.

By validating credentials early, confirming scopes and roles, and following a structured diagnostic approach, you can resolve these issues quickly and prevent them from resurfacing in production.

For more details, see the Zendesk documentation on API token access, OAuth authentication, the Zendesk App Framework, and Messaging JWT authentication.

articles.name: Why is the Summary field blank for some tickets in Explore?
articles.title: Why is the Summary field blank for some tickets in Explore?
articles.body:
Question

Why is the Summary field blank for some tickets in Explore?

Answer

Zendesk stores the AI-generated ticket summaries in ticket fields when an agent manually generates them through the option View ticket summary, or when an agent updates them through the option Refresh. For messaging and chat tickets, the summary populates automatically when the conversation ends or becomes inactive.

If a ticket becomes closed before the Summary field updates, the summary will display in the ticket in Support, but the Summary field and its related attributes will remain blank in Explore.

articles.name: Do I get Copilot with AI Agents - Advanced?
articles.title: Do I get Copilot with AI Agents - Advanced?
articles.body:
Question

Is Copilot included in the AI Agents - Advanced add-on?

Answer

No. AI Agents - Advanced and Copilot are distinct add-ons. Buy Copilot separately. 

Here’s how they differ:

  • Copilot acts as an AI assistant for human agents. It boosts productivity with features such as ticket summaries and reply suggestions.
  • AI Agents - Advanced are autonomous virtual agents that work directly with customers. They manage customer conversations and resolve issues on their own.

Think of Copilot as an automation and a productivity tool for your team. AI Agents - Advanced are a way to automate customer interactions. The Zendesk pricing page contains details.

articles.name: Scheduled Maintenance - March 4-27, 2026 | Zendesk QA
articles.title: Scheduled Maintenance - March 4-27, 2026 | Zendesk QA
articles.body:
Zendesk will perform critical maintenance which will impact Zendesk QA availability for some customers in March, 2026, during the days listed below.

Affected products: Zendesk QA

Important: Only Zendesk QA customers who have been notified via in-product banners will be impacted, based on the maintenance dates and timings below. These banners will appear for account owners and admins in Zendesk Support approximately 14 days before the scheduled Maintenance date listed below. If you do not receive this alert, your account will not be impacted.
Customers Affected: Zendesk QA customers on multiple Pods will experience a maximum service disruption of 30 minutes at some time within the scheduled day of maintenance (the scheduled day of maintenance will be displayed in the aforementioned banner in Support).

Expected behavior: During the migration process, all QA functionalities will be temporarily unavailable for up to 30 minutes.

What do customers have to do?

After the maintenance, if you experience issues accessing QA, try clearing your browser cache and cookies. Please contact Zendesk Support with questions or concerns if you experience other issues.

Why we're doing this

The Zendesk QA team is upgrading infrastructure to enhance the scalability and reliability of our QA services. We understand that this may cause some inconvenience, and we appreciate your patience as we work to improve our services.

articles.name: Service Incident - January 30, 2026 - Explore | All Pods - Some Explore Datasets Not Refreshing
articles.title: Service Incident - January 30, 2026 - Explore | All Pods - Some Explore Datasets Not Refreshing
articles.body:
Summary

On January 30, 2026, from 15:10 UTC to 16:54 UTC, some customers experienced delays in receiving the latest updates in certain Explore features. While the overall service remained available, the information displayed during this time was not up to date.

Timeline

January 30, 2026, 04:59:24 PM UTC | January 30, 2026, 08:59:24 AM PST
Our engineers have identified the issue causing some Explore data sets to not receive updated data and have released the fix. Data that was missed during this period will appear upon the next Explore data refresh. Thank you for your patience.

January 30, 2026, 03:32:00 PM UTC | January 30, 2026, 07:32:00 AM PST
The impacted data sets include: Guide Generative Search, Guide Page Efficiency Analytics, Guide User Session Analytics, AI Suggestions, Intelligent Triage, and AI Auto Assist.

January 30, 2026, 03:23:24 PM UTC | January 30, 2026, 07:23:24 AM PST

We are investigating some Explore data sets for customers in all Pods not receiving updated data in the last two hours. We will provide additional updates shortly.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a recent update that unintentionally stopped the system from properly clearing temporary resources. As a result, unused resources built up, putting extra strain on the infrastructure. This caused some key services to repeatedly restart, which prevented fresh data from being processed and updated.

Resolution

To fix this issue, the team reversed the recent update to a previous stable version, allowing the system to clear unused resources properly. They also increased memory limits for certain components to prevent crashes and temporarily reduced the load on some supporting services to help the recovery process. After these steps, the system stabilized, the affected service recovered, and data updates resumed as normal.

Remediation Items

  1. Set up monitoring and alerts to quickly identify issues that prevent the system from clearing unused resources.

  2. Added alerts to detect high memory usage and crashes in key services for faster response.

  3. Increased memory limits for certain services to help them handle busy times more reliably.

  4. Improved system logging to make it easier to spot errors and delays.

  5. Introduced basic testing of resource creation and removal processes to catch potential problems before releases.

articles.name: Service Incident - January 28, 2026 - Multiple Services  | Pods 18, 20, and 29 - Issue Impacting Multiple Zendesk Functions
articles.title: Service Incident - January 28, 2026 - Multiple Services  | Pods 18, 20, and 29 - Issue Impacting Multiple Zendesk Functions
articles.body:
Summary

On January 28, 2026, from 07:37 UTC to 08:53 UTC, customers on Pods 18, 20, and 29 experienced outages affecting Zendesk Analytics dashboards, Chat, Admin Center, and the product tray. During this time, agents were unable to respond to WhatsApp messages.

Timeline

January 28, 2026, 10:18 AM UTC | January 28, 2026, 2:18 AM PST

We are pleased to inform you that the issues impacting multiple functionalities within Support, Chat, Analytics, and Admin Center on Pods 18, 20, and 29 have been resolved. We appreciate your patience and understanding during the resolution process.

January 28, 2026, 08:16 AM UTC | January 28, 2026, 12:16 AM PST

We are aware of an issue affecting multiple functions within Support, Chat, Analytics and Admin Center. Our engineers have implemented a mitigation, and we are observing signs of recovery. We apologize for the inconvenience and will provide updates as we continue to monitor system stability.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a recent update that accidentally removed a key setting from one of our internal services responsible for managing user access. During this update, a cleanup step mistakenly deleted parts of that service in some areas, which prevented it from working properly and caused outages.

Resolution

To fix this issue, the engineering team reversed the recent update to the last stable version. This restored the affected services, lowered errors, and allowed everything to return to normal. The team continued monitoring closely to make sure everything stayed stable before concluding the incident.

Remediation Items

  1. Prevent teams from making changes that could cause confusion about which resources are in use.

  2. Set up automatic recovery for key services to minimize downtime.

  3. Improve alerts and monitoring around update processes to catch problems sooner.

  4. Review and update procedures to ensure cleanup steps are done properly.

articles.name: How do I reset my password when I can't sign in to the AI agents - Advanced dashboard?
articles.title: How do I reset my password when I can't sign in to the AI agents - Advanced dashboard?
articles.body:
Question

I can't sign in to the AI agents - Advanced dashboard because I forgot my password. How do I reset it?

Answer

To reset your password:

  1. Go to https://dashboard.us.ultimate.ai/signin and enter your email address in the email field. Click Login.

  2. Click Forgot password?

  3. Follow the prompts. Enter your email address, then click Send email.

    You'll receive an email with instructions to reset your password.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-02-06
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-02-06
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Admin Center
  • Copilot
  • AI Agents and Knowledge
  • Voice
  • App Marketplace
  • Zendesk QA
  • Products with no updates this week

Admin Center

New:

  • We’re making some changes to the default behavior of new global OAuth clients so that access and refresh tokens automatically expire. Additionally, the current deadline for existing local (non-global) OAuth clients to adopt the refresh token flow has been extended to April 1, 2027. See Working with OAuth refresh tokens.
  • As a result of the removal of Password access for APIs, customers who were previously ineligible will now be enrolled in two-step verification (2SV). Between February 23 and March 5, 2026, all newly eligible customers with Zendesk authentication turned on will be enrolled in 2SV. 
  • Zendesk is extending permissions for viewing access and audit logs beyond the admin role to include custom roles. Previously, only admin users could audit system activity by accessing these logs. See Creating custom roles and assigning agents.

Updates to product navigation

Support

  • Fixed an issue where top navigation apps displayed only the app name and an 'X' button.
  • Fixed an issue related to Top‑bar navigation where several apps (Aircall, Five9, Amazon Connect, and other third‑party apps) failed or behaved incorrectly, restored full app popouts, foreground/focus/notification handling, and control‑panel loading.
  • Fixed right‑click/context‑menu paste not working in search (search field losing focus or closing).
  • Fixed a search-bar UI bug where the bar closed immediately when clicking or using paste.

Admin Center

  • Fixed an issue preventing options from opening in a new browser tab (impacted workflows that require checking multiple triggers/automations in separate tabs).
  • Fixed an issue in Unified Navigation that prevented opening admin options (e.g., triggers, automations) in new tabs/windows, and restored multi‑tab workflows for checking multiple settings simultaneously.

Knowledge

  • Fixed an issue where the "Edit article" button was not visible to agents when viewing Knowledge Base articles.
  • Fixed an issue where switching brands in Knowledge with the (new navigation) redirected to the Knowledge articles admin page instead of keeping the Help Center view.
  • Fixed an issue in Knowledge admin where the Save button and revision status could disappear when switching languages, risking loss of unsaved changes and requiring a refresh.

Copilot

New:

  • You'll now have the ability to insert public help center articles and published procedures into an existing procedure. See About help center content in procedures and Creating procedures for auto assist.
  • You can now access entity reporting directly within the Intelligent Triage dashboard in Analytics. See Analyzing your intelligent triage activity. 
  • Added support for the Facebook Message ticket channel.

AI Agents and Knowledge

New:

  • Improved request list experience in help center is GA: Help center announcement

Fixed:

  • Sandbox Provisioning - A new capability to provision Sandbox orgs and AI agents is now available for all new customers of AI Agents Advanced
  • Fixed a bug that caused system action in the AI agents - Advanced conversation log to appear as deleted actions.
  • Fixed multiple visual and validation errors in the AI agents - Advanced segment configuration modal.
  • Fixed a bug that resulted in saving errors for AI agents - Advanced dialogues, if they contained conditional blocks using segments.

Voice

New:

  • The Voice call bar, the call controls located at the top of the ticket during the call, has been updated. In addition to a sleeker design, we added a blinking icon to show when the call is being recorded.

Analytics

New:

  • We've added a new quick answers dataset and quick answers tab to the pre-canned Zendesk Knowledge dashboard.
  • We've updated the functionality for reporting on tags. Moving forward, only the functions INCLUDES_ALL and INCLUDES_ANY are supported for use with tags in reports.

App Marketplace

New:
  • Aisera Assist (Support)
    • Aisera Assist works with existing solutions to deliver an end-to-end AI Service Experience for customers. AI Customer Service provides omni-channel integrations through Conversational AI, NLU Search, and unsupervised NLP to speed remediation of knowledge requests and automate the resolution of tasks and actions. Aisera's Assist product brings intelligent automation to classify and route high volumes of tickets, improve resolution times, and user satisfaction. Assist empowers agents by recommending similar tickets, knowledge articles, and next-best actions.
  • Ascend (Support)
    • Ascend is a full-featured UCaaS solution that combines cloud PBX voice, chat, business SMS, video conferencing, file sharing, and contact center into a single platform. Seamlessly integrate key functionalities of Ascend into Zendesk to increase employee productivity and deliver a more personalized experience. Easily place calls using Ascend directly within Zendesk, pull up existing customer tickets on incoming calls, and quickly create support tickets for existing customers.

Zendesk QA

New:

  • For custom prompt testing, we now have session-based prompt test history available. This lets customers view versions of prompts during testing and select the best one to go live with.
    When in a Prompt Testing session, you edit a prompt and click Validate, a new prompt version is recorded along with its evaluation results (match rate and any manual assessments)
    You can view earlier versions and their evaluated results, then choose the most suitable prompt to create.

Products with no updates this week

  • Support
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Messaging and live chat
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)

articles.name: Announcing redesigned Premier plans
articles.title: Announcing redesigned Premier plans
articles.body:
On February 19th, Zendesk launched redesigned Premier plans that deliver greater access to enhanced technical expertise and consulting, 24/7 global coverage (English only), priority routing, and SLA-backed guaranteed response times and in accordance with customers’ license agreements.

This announcement includes the following topics:

  • What is changing?
  • Why is Zendesk making this change?
  • What do I need to do?

What is changing?

We’ve renamed and redesigned the Premier plans to provide even more value than before. The new plans drive tangible outcomes, reduce risk, and scale with your business. Want more than standard Suite support? See how Premier can help and compare plans in the PDF attached at the end of this announcement.

Key changes by plan:

  • Premier Essentials → Premier Access
    • Now includes the Expert Access Catalog.
    • Customers can request unlimited Expert Access assessments (one at a time).
  • Premier Support → Premier Plus 
    • Now includes unlimited Expert Access assessments and interactive workshops  (one at a time)  that provide collaborative, hands-on configuration and guidance for efficiency, security, routing, analytics, and self-service.
    • Added technical strategy and planning with a Technical Account Manager (TAM).
  • Premier Enterprise → Premier Enterprise EAC
    • The name remains the same but now includes the Expert Access Catalog with increased entitlement to unlimited assessments and workshops (two at a time).

Why is Zendesk making this change?

We want to help you realize value faster, increase product adoption, reduce downtime, and deliver measurable ROI. The redesigned plans align support and technical expertise to be more proactive and outcome-driven.

What do I need to do?

All customers currently on Legacy Premier plans are eligible to move to the redesigned Premier plans and receive the additional benefits.

If you're on a Legacy Premier plan, no immediate action is required and you can continue to stay on your current plan. Zendesk will notify you when your subscription is up for renewal with details about the transition.

If you are not currently a Premier customer and would like to learn more, contact your Zendesk Account Team to discuss the right Premier plan for you.

If you have questions or concerns about the transition, or would like to upgrade mid term, please reach out to your Zendesk Account Team.

If you have feedback or questions related to this announcement, visit our community forum where we collect and manage customer product feedback. For general assistance with your Zendesk products, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: Service Incident - January 28, 2026 - Support | Pod 29 - Service Disruption Affecting Side Conversations and Archived Ticket Retrieval
articles.title: Service Incident - January 28, 2026 - Support | Pod 29 - Service Disruption Affecting Side Conversations and Archived Ticket Retrieval
articles.body:
SUMMARY

On January 28, 2026, from 16:48  UTC to 18:15 PM UTC, customers on Pod 29 experienced service disruptions affecting key Support workflows. Customers were unable to load archived tickets, run incremental exports, or access side conversations within Support.

Timeline

January 28, 2026, 06:30 PM UTC | January 28, 2026, 10:30 AM PST

We are happy to report that the issue affecting multiple services on Pod 29 has been resolved, and Side Conversations and Archived Ticket retrieval are functioning as expected at this time. Thank you for your patience during our investigation.

January 28, 2026, 06:02 PM UTC | January 28, 2026, 10:02 AM PST

We are beginning to see recovery from the issue affecting a number of services on Pod 29, including Side Conversations and Archived Ticket retrieval. We are monitoring the situation to ensure full recovery, and will provide further information when we have a substantive update to share.

January 28, 2026,5:41 PM UTC | January 28, 2026, 9:41 AM PST

We are aware of an issue with a partner cloud provider affecting a number of services for customers on Pod 29, including Side Conversations and Archived Ticket access. We are working with them to mitigate impact and will provide additional information as the situation progresses.

January 28, 2026, 5:28 PM UTC | January 28, 2026, 9:28 AM PST

We are receiving reports of side conversation and archived ticket access issues for customers on Pod 29. We are investigating and will provide further updates shortly.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a delay in updates within a network system that affected connectivity. As a result, some services on Pod 29 were unable to connect to a key database, impacting their ability to load archived tickets, side conversations, and other related Support features.

Resolution

To fix this issue, we reached out to our cloud service provider for assistance and raised the issue within our team. The provider addressed the network delay, restoring normal connections. After confirming the fix, all affected features were fully restored, and customers regained access.

Remediation Items

  1. Review and adjust alert settings for connection issues to catch similar problems earlier.

  2. Investigate why only Pod 29 was affected while others in the same region were not, to understand any differences.

  3. Work with our cloud provider to improve communication by reconnecting with a dedicated support representative.

  4. Improve alert systems related to side conversation problems to detect and respond to incidents more quickly.

articles.name: Managing limited Copilot usage
articles.title: Managing limited Copilot usage
articles.body:
To help you understand the benefits ofCopilotand get familiar with how it works, all Zendesk Suite and Support Professional and above plans include limited access to the Copilot generative AI writing tools.

What's my plan?
All Suites Professional, Enterprise, or Enterprise Plus
Support Professional or Enterprise

Verified AI summary ◀▼

Explore the benefits of Copilot's AI writing tools, which help enhance ticket comments by expanding, simplifying, or adjusting tone. Each agent gets five uses per month, pooled across your team. Monitor usage in Admin Center and upgrade if needed. Consider a 30-day trial or purchase the full add-on for unlimited access and additional features.

To help you understand the benefits of Copilot and get familiar with how it works, all Zendesk Suite and Support Professional and above plans include limited access to the Copilot generative AI writing tools.

This article describes the plan requirements, your monthly usage allowance, how to monitor your account's usage, and what to do when you reach your usage limit.

This article includes the following sections:

  • Understanding the Copilot writing tools
  • Understanding monthly Copilot usage limits
  • Monitoring your account's Copilot AI usage
  • Upgrading to the full Copilot add-on
Related articles:
  • Buying the Copilot add-on

Understanding the Copilot writing tools

The generative AI writing tools are available within the ticket interface and help agents enhance the content of ticket comments in different ways:

  • Expand: Rephrases agent comments, help center content, or macro content with additional language.
  • Simplify: Rephrases agent comments, help center content, or macro content to be more concise and focus on the main idea.
  • Make more friendly: Changes the tone of an agent comment, selected help center content, or macro content so that it sounds more conversational.
  • Make more formal: Changes the tone of an agent comment, selected help center content, or macro content so that it sounds more professional.
  • Write a custom prompt: Changes the content of an agent comment based on the custom instructions entered to enhance or adjust text.

By default, writing tools are turned on and all groups have access. See Turning on the enhance writing generative AI feature to learn more and manage access.

Understanding monthly Copilot usage limits

Copilot generative AI writing tools are available in a limited capacity on all Suite and Support Professional plans and above.

Your usage allowance is five writing tool uses per agent, per month:

  • Usage is pooled across all agents in your instance, so team members can share the monthly allowance as needed.
  • The allowance is calculated as the number of agents multiplied by 5 uses per agent, per month. For example, if you have 10 agents, your team will have 50 uses available each month.
  • If you add more agent seats during the month, then your usage limit is instantly updated. For example, if you add 10 additional agents then your account receives 50 more uses.
  • When the monthly usage limit is reached, access to the feature is paused until the next billing cycle. Agents are notified in the ticket interface.

    You'll see notifications in Admin Center when you're nearing your monthly allowance and when you've reached it.

    To continue using the writing tools after you reach your usage limit, you can upgrade anytime to the full Copilot add-on.

 

Monitoring your account's Copilot AI usage

You can monitor your Copilot AI usage to determine how close you are to your monthly usage allowance.

To monitor your Copilot AI usage
  1. In Admin Center, click Account in the sidebar, then select Usage > Summary.

    The Copilot Monthly usage box displays the number of writing tool uses in your account this month, and the number remaining.

  2. To learn more about Copilot features, click Overview.

    If you're a billing admin, you can also start a trial or buy the Copilot add-on from this page.

Upgrading to the full Copilot add-on

After your agents get familiar with the Copilot writing tools, you may find that you're frequently reaching your Copilot usage limits or that you’re curious about additional Copilot features. In this case, you can:
  • Start a 30-day free trial of the Copilot add-on from the Copilot Overview page or Launchpad.
  • Purchase the Copilot add-on directly from your account. See Instantly buying the Copilot add-on.

articles.name: I can't access the Zendesk QA Chrome extension
articles.title: I can't access the Zendesk QA Chrome extension
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When I try to access the Zendesk QA Chrome extension, I see a blank page.

Resolution steps

You may have used a previous login method tied to a former Klaus account to sign in to the Zendesk QA Chrome extension. Now, the extension may not load, show a blank page, or crash your browser.

To authenticate the extension again, follow these steps:

  • Clear your browser’s cached data and cookies for All time
  • Restart the browser, then load the Zendesk QA extension again
  • Select Continue with Zendesk
  • Enter your Zendesk subdomain, then click Sign in

For more information about this extension, see this article: Setting up and using the Zendesk QA Chrome browser extension.

articles.name: Service Incident - January 29, 2026 - Support | Multiple Pods -Web Widget scrolling issues
articles.title: Service Incident - January 29, 2026 - Support | Multiple Pods -Web Widget scrolling issues
articles.body:
SUMMARY

On January 29, 2026 from 16:32 UTC  to 16:52 UTC, customers experienced issues with the Zendesk Web Widget where the scrollbar was not visible or functioning correctly. This prevented users from scrolling within the widget, thereby blocking their ability to continue conversations or submit responses. 

Timeline

January 29, 2026, 5:02 PM UTC | January 29, 2026, 9:02 AM PST

The issue with the scrollbar in the Zendesk Support Web Widget has been resolved. Our engineering team has reverted a recent change, and we kindly ask you to refresh your browser and verify if the scrollbar is now functioning properly.

January 29, 2026, 4:43 PM UTC | January 29, 2026, 8:43 AM PST

We are aware of an issue with Zendesk Support where the scrollbar in the Web Widget is not displaying or functioning properly for customers. This affects users’ ability to continue conversations and communicate with agents. Our engineering team is currently investigating the matter. We apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused and appreciate your understanding.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a recent update intended to improve video playback within the widget. This update modified the way the widget’s content area was structured, which unintentionally affected how the widget displayed the scroll bar, preventing it from working properly.

Resolution

To fix this issue, the recent update that caused the scrollbar issue was reversed, allowing the scrolling feature in the Web Widget to work normally again. Customers were asked to refresh their browsers to see the changes.

Remediation Items

  1. Allow sufficient time between deployment phases to minimize the number of customers affected, especially when rolling out changes in stages.

articles.name: Scheduled Maintenance - February 26-27, 2026 | Pods 13, 15, 19, 20, 23, 25, 26, 27, 30 | Profiles API and Events API
articles.title: Scheduled Maintenance - February 26-27, 2026 | Pods 13, 15, 19, 20, 23, 25, 26, 27, 30 | Profiles API and Events API
articles.body:
Zendesk will perform critical maintenance which will impact performance for customers using the Profiles and Events APIs on multiple Pods from February 26 to February 27, 2026, during the times listed below.

Affected products: 

  • Zendesk Sunshine Platform - Custom Data - Profiles API (Profiles)
  • Zendesk Sunshine Platform - Custom Data - Events API (Events)
Important: Only customers who have been notified via in-product banners will be impacted, based on the Pod maintenance dates and timings below. These banners will appear for account owners and admins in Zendesk Support on February 12, 2026. If you do not receive this alert, your account will not be impacted.
Customers Affected: Zendesk Profiles API and Events API customers on all Pods will experience a maximum service disruption of 30 minutes at some time within one of the maintenance windows below.

Expected behavior: Profiles API and Events API will face elevated latency. As a result requests made to the API (i.e. create, read, update or delete requests) may experience delays, errors or timeouts during the maintenance window.

What do customers have to do? During these upcoming maintenance windows, you may experience delays or errors when sending requests to the impacted services. To avoid any interruptions, we recommend holding off on sending requests during this time.

If your applications are configured with built-in retry mechanisms, please make sure those settings are active. Please keep in mind that it may still take longer than usual or not work as expected while we complete the maintenance work. Once it’s done, Profiles API and Events API performance should return to normal, and retrying your requests should succeed at this point.

Why we're doing this: The Zendesk User’s Platform team is upgrading our database to strengthen security and reliability.

articles.name: Configuring number assignments and voicemail for direct agent routing
articles.title: Configuring number assignments and voicemail for direct agent routing
articles.body:
Direct agent routing gives customers and partners a fast path to the right person, reduces time in shared queues, and makes ownership crystal clear. You can use it to offer VIP access to specialists, streamline internal and partner calls, and capture after-hours messages with voicemail for timely followups.

What's my plan?
Add-on Zendesk for Contact Center

Direct agent routing gives customers and partners a fast path to the right person, reduces time in shared queues, and makes ownership crystal clear. You can use it to offer VIP access to specialists, streamline internal and partner calls, and capture after-hours messages with voicemail for timely followups.

To achieve this, admins configure back office number assignments for agents. Back office number assignments can be either direct phone numbers know as direct inward dialing (DID) or internal extensions. Additionally, you can choose whether to offer voicemail for back office numbers.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Setting up a direct number for an agent
  • Setting up an extension for an agent
Related articles:
  • Configuring user-based voicemail

Setting up a direct number for an agent

When setting up a dedicated phone number for an agent using DID, you can choose whether to offer voicemail and a custom message on that number.

To assign a direct number to an agent
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Backoffice tab at the top.
  4. Click New DID Configuration.
  5. In the new DID configuration, select the Agent this phone number will route calls to.

    This is the DID owner.

  6. Enter the dedicated Phone number for this agent.
  7. (Optional) Select Enable voicemail to offer voicemail for this number when the agent is unavailable.
  8. (Optional) Select Use custom message to play a custom voicemail message for this number when the agent is unavailable.
  9. (Optional) Under Voicemail language, select the language to use for the voicemail prompt.
  10. (Optional) Enter the custom Voicemail message to play to callers.
  11. (Optional) Select Enable call recording to record calls to this number.
  12. Click Create configuration.

Setting up an extension for an agent

When setting up an internal extension for an agent, you can control whether callers can leave voicemail on that extension and, if so, configure voicemail settings such as language and custom prompt options.

To assign an extension to an agent
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Backoffice tab at the top.
  4. Click New Extension Configuration.
  5. In the new extension configuration, select the Agent this extension will route to.

    This is the extension owner.

  6. Enter the dedicated Extension number for this agent (for example, 1001 or 2001).
  7. (Optional) Select Enable voicemail to offer voicemail for this number when the agent is unavailable.
  8. (Optional) Select Use custom message to play a custom voicemail message for this number when the agent is unavailable.
  9. (Optional) Under Voicemail language, select the language to use for the voicemail prompt.
  10. (Optional) Enter the custom Voicemail message to play to callers.
  11. (Optional) Select Enable call recording to record calls to this number.
  12. Click Create configuration.

articles.name: Configuring voicemail in Contact Center
articles.title: Configuring voicemail in Contact Center
articles.body:
Voicemail in Zendesk Contact Center captures caller messages and delivers them as Zendesk tickets with audio playback and optional transcription. Settings are configured in the admin interface and applied at runtime in Amazon Connect contact flows.

What's my plan?
Add-on Zendesk for Contact Center

Voicemail in Zendesk Contact Center captures caller messages and delivers them as Zendesk tickets with audio playback and optional transcription. Settings are configured in the admin interface and applied at runtime in Amazon Connect contact flows.

You can configure voicemail for inbound calls to reduce missed calls and create clear ownership when agents or queues are unavailable. Voicemail can be configured per queue or per agent using direct inward dialing (DID) or extension numbers. You can also control how voicemail tickets are created in Zendesk, and optionally include transcriptions.

Admins can access voicemail settings in the Zendesk for Contact Center admin interface. If you can't access the settings, then contact Zendesk Customer Support to request the activation of the Voicemail solution on your CloudFormation stack.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Understanding voicemail in Contact Center
  • Configuring queue-based voicemail
  • Configuring user-based voicemail

Understanding voicemail in Contact Center

There are two ways to deliver voicemail, depending on whether messages should be worked by a shared team or by a specific agent:

  • Contact Center queue-based voicemail: Voicemails are delivered to a Zendesk group associated with an Amazon Connect queue. Use this for shared contact center queues.
  • User-based voicemail: Voicemails are delivered to a specific agent workflow. You’ll select the agent who owns the direct back office number (DID owner) or extension and the agent who should be assigned the resulting ticket. This can be the same person or different people.

Voicemail tickets include caller details, call metadata, an audio recording with playback, and, (if activated) a transcription. They behave like standard Zendesk tickets, so you can use existing views, triggers, automations, and reporting.

Configuring queue-based voicemail

Use a queue-based voicemail configuration when callers should leave messages for a shared team. Voicemails are delivered to a Zendesk group associated with an Amazon Connect queue.

To configure queue-based voicemail
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Voicemail tab at the top to open voicemail settings.
  4. Under Contact Center, click Add new configuration.

  5. Under Origin queue, select the Amazon Connect queue this voicemail configuration applies to.
  6. Select the Zendesk assignable group to which newly created voicemail tickets will be assigned.
  7. Under Zendesk Tags, select one or more ticket tags to add to these voicemail tickets.

    These tags can be used for routing, automation, and reporting.

  8. (Optional) Select Enable transcription to include an automated voicemail transcription in the tickets.
  9. Click Add configuration.
Tip: Use ticket tags and triggers to automatically route voicemail tickets to the correct groups.

Configuring user-based voicemail

Use a user-based (or agent-based) voicemail configuration to assign tickets resulting from missed calls to an agent’s direct number to a specific agent for follow-up. The DID owner and the ticket assignee can be the same agent, buto don't have to be.

To configure user-based voicemail
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Voicemail tab at the top to open the voicemail settings.
  4. Click the Back Office tab and then click Add new configuration.

  5. Under User, select the agent who owns the direct number this voicemail configuration applies to.

    This is the DID owner. To add user entries, see Configuring number assignments and voicemail for direct agent routing.

  6. Under Zendesk assignee agent, select the Zendesk agent to assign the voicemail tickets created from calls to this DID.

  7. Under Zendesk tags, select one or more ticket tags to add to these voicemail tickets.

    These tags can be used for routing, automation, and reporting.

  8. (Optional) Select Enable transcription to include an automated voicemail transcription in the tickets.

  9. Click Add configuration.

articles.name: How to Procure Agent Logs in Zendesk for Contact Center
articles.title: How to Procure Agent Logs in Zendesk for Contact Center
articles.body:
Agent logs are a great resource when working with Zendesk Support to diagnose and resolve issues surrounding Zendesk for Contact Center. Zendesk Support may ask for these logs during the investigation, so please view the gif below on how to download them:

It is important to download the logs after the issue you are reporting has been reproduced. 

Note: Agent logs do not persist through browser refreshes. Refreshing the browser or clearing cache and cookies will cause you to lose existing agent logs.

A txt file named agent-log.txt is saved to your browser's default directory. After the file is downloaded, you can change the name of the file the same way you rename any other file on your computer. 

Analyzing the Agent Logs

After downloading the agent logs (also known as Contact Control Panel (CCP) agent logs in Amazon Connect), navigate Amazon Connect's CCP Log Parser to analyze them by dragging and dropping the downloaded agent-log.txt file:

On the Snapshots & Logs tab you will see the logs recorded during the agent session, filterable by the type of log. It's important to note that these logs do not persist through browser refreshes.

Normally log entries are collapsed but most log entries contain more information. To see the original log object in JSON format, click the + to expand or collapse the log lines with more information. 

 

On the left side of log entry, you can choose Snapshots, which are periodic. The Snapshot displays the agent status that was captured during retrieval periods. Clicking on one Snapshot highlights the section from that snapshot until the subsequent snapshot.

The following image shows a Snapshot log, with a softphone error.

On the Metrics tab, you can view the following metrics:

  • Skew Metrics shows difference between the client-side (agent's workstation) local timestamp and server-side (Amazon Connect service) timestamp in milliseconds.
  • API Call Metrics shows the latency of the API call from CCP.
  • WebRTC Metrics: Available if the call was made with CCP. WebRTC Metrics shows the media stream condition during a call.

  1.

articles.name: Creating and configuring contact center business units
articles.title: Creating and configuring contact center business units
articles.body:
In Contact Center, business units allow you to control contact center availability, emergency closures, and public holidays. They allow you to group queues together and apply closure rules consistently across departments and individual queues without modifying individual contact flows.

What's my plan?
Add-on Zendesk for Contact Center

In Contact Center, business units allow you to control contact center availability, emergency closures, and public holidays. They allow you to group queues together and apply closure rules consistently across departments and individual queues without modifying individual contact flows.

Admins can access the business unit settings in the Zendesk for Contact Center admin interface. If you can't access the settings, then contact Zendesk Customer Support to request the activation of the Business Unit solution on your CloudFormation stack.

This article contains the following topics:

  • Understanding business units
  • Creating business units
  • Adding queues to business units
  • Adding public holidays

Understanding business units

A business unit represents a logical department or area of your contact center and groups one or more Amazon Connect queues under a single operational profile.

With business units admins can:

  • Apply emergency closures at the business unit level (affects all associated queues) or at an individual queue level within the unit.
  • Define public holidays per business unit to manage non-standard operating days.

Creating business units

Create a business unit to group related queues under a shared operational profile. Business unit settings are evaluated at runtime within Amazon Connect contact flows.

To create a business unit
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Business Units tab at the top.
  4. Click Add new business unit.
  5. Enter a unique name that identifies the business unit.

  6. (Optional) Enter a description to clarify the unit’s scope and purpose.
  7. (Optional) Configure business unit–level emergency closure settings:
    • Emergency close: Activate to stop call handling for all queues in the unit.
    • Close message: Add a message to play to callers when the unit is closed.
  8. Select Create business unit.

Adding queues to business units

Add one or more Amazon Connect queues to a business unit so the unit’s emergency closure and public holiday settings apply to those queues. Each queue can be associated with only one business unit.

To add a queue to a business unit
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Business Units tab at the top.
  4. Click the name of the business unit you want to update.
  5. Under Manage Queues and Holidays, click Add queue.
  6. Select a queue to assign to the business unit.

    You can assign a queue to only one business unit. To change a queue's business unit, you must first unassign the queue from the original unit.

  7. (Optional) Set queue–level emergency closure:
    • Emergency close: Activate to stop call handling for this queue.
    • Close message: Add a message to play to callers when this queue is closed.
  8. Cick Add queue

Adding public holidays

Configure public holidays to manage non-standard operating days for each business unit. Public holidays are evaluated at runtime and can be combined with hours of operation logic in contact flows.

To add public holidays for a business unit
  1. In Contact Center, click Admin Settings.
  2. Click the settings icon () in the sidebar.
  3. Click the Business Units tab at the top.
  4. Click the name of the business unit you want to update.
  5. Under Manage Queues and Holidays, click Add holiday.
  6. Enter the holiday details:
    • Holiday Name: A short, descriptive name.
    • Holiday Date: The calendar date for the holiday.
    • Closed: Activate to close the business unit for the full day.
    • Close message: The message played to callers when the unit is closed for this holiday.

  7. Click Add holiday.
Tip: Holidays are specific to the selected business unit and apply to full days. If you need reduced hours, leave Closed off and set hours-of-operation in the contact flow.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-01-30
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-01-30
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Support
  • AI Agents and Knowledge
  • Voice
  • App Marketplace
  • Products with no updates this week

Support

New:

  • We're adding three new abilities to the print tickets feature:
    • Include side conversations (Zendesk plans that include side conversations only)
    • Print without including internal comments
    • Include the ticket’s priority

  • The original recipient address is now included on follow-up tickets

  • We've introduced a redesigned notifications experience for messaging conversation updates. This notification center will serve as a centralized hub to keep agents up to date on new conversation assignments, new end-user replies, and @mentions from other agents.

AI Agents and Knowledge

New:

  • We've added new API integration observability improvements in the conversation logs for AI agents - Advanced.

Fixed:

  • Fixed a bug that caused the segment labels to break in the dialogue builder if an AI agent had more than 100 segments.
  • Entities presets functionality was fixed
  • Entity detection on email was fixed
  • Fixed navigation from procedure builder to use case details or use case overview page
  • Fixed a bug within AI-generated procedures for Copilot auto assist that used "unavailable" actions

Voice

New 

  • Enhancements to the voicemail transcription model used by Zendesk Voice. This update makes voicemail transcriptions significantly faster and expands transcription language to 35 new languages. Additionally, voicemail transcripts can now be processed by Zendesk Copilot.

App Marketplace

New:
  • Isara (Support)
    • Isara connects seamlessly with Zendesk to automatically analyze every customer conversation in real time, turning support tickets into actionable intelligence for your entire organization.  Monitor critical conversations as they happen. Pulse detects frustration signals, escalation risks, and urgent issues in real time. Insights automatically surfaces recurring issues, product gaps, and feature requests across your entire support history.  Analyze conversation patterns and deliver specific recommendations for product fixes, agent coaching opportunities, help center improvements, and process optimizations.
  • Simplesat (Support)
    • Simplesat adds one-click CSAT, CES, or NPS surveys to Zendesk email triggers in minutes. Simplesat works with Zendesk event-based triggers or time-based automations. By inserting your survey into existing notifications, you avoid sending extra emails to customers.  You can even sync Zendesk ticket details with customer feedback, including thingsl ike private comments and tags.
  • EdgeTier Integration (Support)
    • EdgeTier is an AI-powered conversation intelligence platform that integrates seamlessly with your Zendesk data. By analyzing every ticket, chat, and conversation in real time, EdgeTier identifies issues as they emerge, automatically reviews conversations for quality, and surfaces actionable insights for your team.
  • Thank You Autosolve GPT by Ino Studio (Support)
    • Thank You Autosolve GPT by Ino Studio uses advanced AI (GPT) to detect when customers send simple thank-you or acknowledgment messages that reopen solved tickets. When detected, the app automatically sets the ticket back to solved, keeping your queue clean and reducing agent workload for non-actionable ticket reopens.

Products with no updates this week

  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Copilot
  • Messaging and live chat
  • Mobile
  • Analytics
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Ticket no longer shared
articles.title: Ticket no longer shared
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

After I unshare a ticket, I try to share it again. The system unshares the ticket automatically.

Resolution steps

If you share a ticket within 10 seconds of your last unshare, the system unshares it automatically. It shows a record in the Events tab of the ticket with the message Ticket no longer shared with.

To resolve this issue, wait more than 10 seconds after you unshare, then share the ticket again.

articles.name: Why can't I see Intelligent Triage under AI in the sidebar?
articles.title: Why can't I see Intelligent Triage under AI in the sidebar?
articles.body:
Problem

Why can't I see Intelligent Triage under AI in the sidebar?

Solution

Intelligent Triage is part of Zendesk Advanced AI and requires the paid Copilot add-on and a Suite Professional or Enterprise plan. To resolve this, follow these steps:

  1. Ask your account owner to check if your account includes the Copilot add-on and a Suite Professional or Enterprise plan. See Zendesk plans and prices. Otherwise, the Intelligent Triage page appears blank, even for admins.
  2. Consider an upgrade to your plan or start a 30-day trial

If you recently requested the add-on, there may be a delay. Contact Zendesk customer support to confirm.
